paper.title: Finding the dispersing siblings of young open clusters. Dynamical traceback simulations using Gaia DR3

review.summary: This paper uses Gaia DR3 radial velocity data (6D phase space) to perform Monte Carlo traceback computations for 10 nearby young open clusters, identifying escaped 'fugitive' stars. Core data products: (1) a table of individual membership/fugitive probabilities for Gaia DR3 sources deposited at CDS; (2) summary statistics (fugitive counts, false positive rates) for 10 clusters; (3) cluster age/metallicity reference table. External dependencies: Gaia DR3, SIMBAD (via astroquery), Gala/MWPotential2014 gravitational potential, PARSEC isochrones. No custom catalog or survey data is produced beyond the CDS table.

source_refs.caption: Adopted parameters of the solar orbit.
source_refs.label: tab:solar_parameters

columns.name: Symbol
columns.meaning: Symbol used in text for the parameter

columns.name: Reference
columns.meaning: Literature source for the parameter value

internal_tables.evidence: Parameters consistent with MWPotential2014: z_sun=14pc, solar peculiar velocity, R_sun=8.0kpc, v0=220 km/s.
internal_tables.comments: Used to convert heliocentric Gaia coordinates to galactocentric coordinates for traceback.

source_refs.caption: Limits for the coordinates of the stars included in the initial traceback computation.
source_refs.label: tab:galactocentric_selection

internal_tables.evidence: Spatial/kinematic cuts on Gaia DR3 RV sample. Resulting sample: 4,238,086 sources.
internal_tables.comments: Reduces full Gaia DR3 radial velocity sample to a manageable local volume for traceback.

source_refs.caption: Cluster ages and metallicities from Bossini et al. (2019).
source_refs.label: tab:cluster_parameters

columns.name: Cluster age | Myr
columns.meaning: Age of the cluster with 16th/84th percentiles from Bayesian posterior

columns.name: [Fe/H]
columns.meaning: Iron abundance relative to solar, used for isochrone fitting

internal_tables.evidence: 10 clusters with ages from 27 to ~310 Myr. Most at solar metallicity.
internal_tables.comments: Cluster ages used as maximum traceback time cutoff. Metallicities used for PARSEC isochrone comparison.

source_refs.caption: Membership and fugitive probabilities of the Gaia DR3 sources.
source_refs.label: tab:probabilities

columns.name: Membership probability
columns.meaning: Fraction of Monte Carlo tracebacks where star starts in cluster neighborhood (p_m)

columns.name: Fugitive probability
columns.meaning: Fraction of Monte Carlo tracebacks where star enters cluster neighborhood at any t <= cluster age (p_f)

columns.name: Cluster
columns.meaning: Name of the cluster the star is potentially associated with

internal_tables.evidence: Only first 5 rows shown in paper. Full machine-readable table at CDS.
internal_tables.comments: Primary data product. Full version deposited at CDS. Each star may appear for multiple clusters.

source_refs.caption: False positive rates (medians with 16th/84th percentiles) across Monte Carlo tracebacks.
source_refs.label: tab:cluster_results

columns.name: No. of fugitives
columns.meaning: Number of stars identified as fugitives (p_f > 0.1 >= p_m)

columns.name: No. of members
columns.meaning: Number of stars identified as current members (p_f >= p_m > 0.1)

columns.name: False positive rate | %
columns.meaning: Median field star contamination rate estimated via reference point method

internal_tables.evidence: Fugitives range: 19 (Blanco 1) to 143 (Alpha Per). Median false positive rate ~6%, max 14%.
internal_tables.comments: False positive rates estimated by comparing cluster neighborhood counts to a symmetric reference point.

source_refs.caption: Alternative solar orbit parameters, also used by Meingast et al. (2021).
source_refs.label: tab:alternative_solar_parameters

columns.name: Reference
columns.meaning: Literature source for the alternative value

internal_tables.evidence: Alternative R_sun=8122pc, z_sun=20.8pc. Used in robustness test showing Pearson coefficients ~1.
internal_tables.comments: Demonstrates that fugitive probability results are insensitive to solar orbit parameter choices.

source_refs.caption: Rough cluster coordinates from SIMBAD used as initial guesses.
source_refs.label: tab:rough_cluster_coordinates

columns.name: mu_alpha* | mas yr^-1
columns.meaning: Proper motion in right ascension (cos(delta) corrected)

internal_tables.evidence: SIMBAD queried coordinates for 10 clusters used as initial guess for iterative refinement.
internal_tables.comments: Appendix A. Exact values non-critical -- refined using median of neighborhood stars.

external_resources.description: Full machine-readable version of Table tab:probabilities -- Gaia DR3 source IDs, coordinates, membership probabilities (p_m), fugitive probabilities (p_f), and associated cluster for all stars with p_f > 0.

source_refs.context: The full table is available in machine-readable form at the CDS.
The full table that contains all stars with positive fugitive probabilities is available at the CDS.

external_resources.evidence: Explicit statements that the complete probability table is deposited at CDS.
external_resources.comments: Primary data product. Accessible via CDS/VizieR associated with the published A&A article.

external_resources.description: Gaia DR3 radial velocity subsample -- full 6D phase space information for ~34 million sources. Initial sample of 4,238,086 sources after galactocentric cuts.

source_refs.context: Gaia DR3 has published full six-dimensional phase space information

external_resources.evidence: Gaia DR3 is the sole observational data source for stellar kinematics used in traceback.
external_resources.comments: Parallax zero-point offset corrected. Sources with corrected parallax < 1 mas discarded.

external_resources.description: SIMBAD astronomical database queried via astroquery for initial cluster coordinates used as initial guesses.

source_refs.context: cluster coordinates were obtained by querying SIMBAD with astroquery

external_resources.evidence: SIMBAD used for initial cluster coordinate guesses, listed in Appendix tab:rough_cluster_coordinates.
external_resources.comments: Exact values not critical -- refined iteratively.

external_resources.description: Gala Python package -- leapfrog integrator used for traceback computations in BovyMWPotential2014 gravitational potential.

source_refs.context: Traceback computations using a leapfrog integrator implemented in Gala

external_resources.evidence: Entire traceback computation pipeline relies on Gala and MWPotential2014.
external_resources.comments: Timestep: 0.25 Myr. Maximum traceback time: 100 Myr.

external_resources.description: PARSEC stellar evolutionary isochrones used for HR diagram validation of identified members and fugitives.
external_resources.evidence: PARSEC isochrones used for validation: fugitives match isochrones as well as cluster members.
external_resources.comments: Not a data product of this paper -- used for validation only.

external_resources.description: Astropy core Python package and astroquery used for SIMBAD queries and data processing.

source_refs.context: This work made use of astropy: a community-developed core Python package

external_resources.evidence: Acknowledged in acknowledgements section along with matplotlib and numpy.
external_resources.comments: General-purpose astronomy software; astroquery used for SIMBAD queries.
